compiled_state.title: 3-Gate Task Granularity Framework for Implementation Plan Skill
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
When DSO practitioners run /dso:sprint to execute epics, the implementation-plan skill generates tasks that sub-agents execute autonomously. Today, these tasks are often larger than necessary — bundling independently verifiable changes into single tasks. This causes three concrete problems: (1) when a sub-agent fails mid-task, the failure signal is ambiguous because the task contains multiple changes, making diagnosis slower; (2) larger tasks consume more context window and have higher failure rates, wasting compute on retries; (3) practitioners reviewing plans cannot easily verify that each task is correctly scoped because the "one concern" rule is subjective. For example, a recent plan bundled --min-children and --max-children flag implementations into one task despite each being independently testable and deployable.

## Success Criteria
1. Step 3 Directives in implementation-plan SKILL.md contain the 3-gate framework as the primary task decomposition rule: Gate 1 (must produce testable behavior — grepping for code existence is not valid), Gate 2 (must leave codebase green with no regression), Gate 3 (must not be possible to split the task into smaller tasks that each independently meet gates 1 and 2)
2. The Task Design reviewer's atomicity dimension in task-design.md scores against the same 3-gate definitions, using identical gate language to prevent interpretation drift between plan generation and review
3. The existing "acceptable bundling" exception is subsumed by Gate 3 — bundling is acceptable only when splitting would violate Gate 1 or Gate 2
4. After delivery, run /dso:implementation-plan on a multi-change story (>=2 independently verifiable behaviors) and confirm: (a) the plan produces separate tasks per verifiable behavior, (b) no task can be further subdivided while meeting gates 1 and 2, and (c) the Task Design reviewer scores atomicity at 5

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Replace the current "Stability" directive and "acceptable bundling" exception in Step 3 Directives with the 3-gate framework. Update the Task Design reviewer's atomicity dimension to score against identical gate definitions. The gates are conjunctive — Gate 3 only mandates splitting when the split would not violate Gate 1 or Gate 2.

## Scenario Analysis
- Gate 1 validator drift between plan generation and review (high, Runtime): Both SKILL.md and task-design.md must use identical gate definitions — if one applies Gate 1 permissively and the other strictly, the reviewer will reject tasks the orchestrator already approved with no convergence path. SC2 addresses this directly by requiring identical language.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 1
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none

comments.body: Epic complete: all tasks closed, review passed (score 5/5), branch pushed
comments.author: Test
